REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT NEW PENN FINANCIAL LLC D/B/A SHELLPOINT MORTGAGE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. VIOLATION OF DEBT COLLECTION LAWS IS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED (SEE PARAGRAPHS 35 AND 36). THESE ACTIVITIES ARE OUTSIDE OF THE FORECLOSURE PROCEEDINGS AND PLAINTIFFS ALLEGATIONS MUST BE TAKEN AS TRUE AT THIS STAGE OF THE LITIGATION. DEFENDANT ADMITS THAT UCL CAUSE OF ACTION SURVIVES WITH THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION BEING A PREDICATE FOR IT. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED LOSS OF MONEY DUE TO UNFAIR/UNLAWFUL PRACTICES. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED VIOLATION OF CC 2923.5 AND THE VERACITY OF THESE ALLEGATIONS IS NOT PROPERLY CHALLENGED. THERE IS AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY REGARDING THE AMOUNTS OWED UNDER THE NOTE/DOT AND PARTIES OBLIGATIONS ON THE CONTRACT GOING FORWARD; AND NO OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION ADDRESS THIS ISSUE. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS GIVING RISE TO THE DUTY. THEREFORE, DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS EACH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE CHARLES HAINES, CLERK S. LE, NOT REPORTED (501)

Off Calendar Nov-05-2019 Vacated

Off Calendar Jun-03-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Set For Trial On Jul-08-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tiffany Norman, esq. (no number provided) for Plaintiff/opposing party Katia Bonilla.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NEW PENN FINANCIAL LLC D/B/A SHELLPOINT MORTGAGE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Violation of debt collection laws is sufficiently alleged (See paragraphs 35 and 36). These activities are outside of the foreclosure proceedings and Plaintiffs allegations must be taken as true at this stage of the litigation. Defendant admits that UCL cause of action survives with the first cause of action being a predicate for it. Plaintiff sufficiently alleged loss of money due to unfair/unlawful practices. Plaintiff sufficiently alleged violation of CC 2923.5 and the veracity of these allegations is not properly challenged. There is an actual controversy regarding the amounts owed under the Note/DOT and parties obligations on the contract going forward; and no other causes of action address this issue. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts giving rise to the duty. Therefore, demurrer is overruled as each cause of action is sufficiently alleged. Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge Charles Haines, Clerk S. Le, not reported (501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2018 Continued To Oct-11-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT NEW PENN FINANCIAL LLC D/B/A SHELLPOINT MORTGAGE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to October 11, 2018 on Court's own motion. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
